rationale: The stalled offensive and loss of strategic positions by Novaris create an opportunity to exploit weakened defenses with precision strikes to degrade their forward capabilities.
Reinforcing our naval presence near Serpent Island deters further escalation by Novaris and secures maritime lines for potential resupply or amphibious operations.
Conducting large-scale exercises near the front signals resolve and maintains troop readiness amid prolonged positional warfare.
The Aggressor’s military failure and high casualties have created logistical and morale vulnerabilities; covert sabotage can disrupt supply chains and prevent regrouping.
Exploit economic strain and battlefield losses by amplifying narratives of leadership incompetence to erode domestic support for the war.
Target command-and-control systems weakened by recent operational failures to delay future offensive planning.
With sanctions at 42% and our economy contracting, securing critical resources now will buffer against deeper economic strain and sustain war efforts.
Leverage strong international support (80%) to coordinate a coalition-backed embargo on key war materials, amplifying economic pressure where sanctions are plateauing.
Raise domestic capital to offset GDP contraction and fund military sustainment without increasing sovereign debt vulnerability.
The stalled Novaris offensive and their heavy losses indicate a vulnerable moment; we must exploit their fatigue by reinforcing our own positions and preparing for limited counteroffensives.
In response to Novaris deploying additional warships, a visible naval show of force near Serpent Island will signal resolve and deter further maritime escalation.
Targeted strikes against overextended logistics hubs will compound their sustainment challenges caused by sanctions and recent battlefield losses.
With Novaris reeling from economic strain and battlefield failures, their cyber defenses are likely overtaxed; a precision cyber operation can disrupt command coordination and delay recovery.
Exploit declining morale and strategic confusion by feeding false intelligence suggesting internal betrayal or imminent Tethyan counteroffensives.
Isolate and target mid-level officers through covert means to erode operational continuity during this critical window of enemy vulnerability.
With 80% international support, now is the moment to solidify coalition unity and coordinate next-phase responses to Novaris’s stalled aggression and naval escalation.
Open discreet channels to probe for fissures within Novaris’s leadership, leveraging their current military and economic strain to explore conditional de-escalation terms.
Propose deployment of neutral monitors to stabilize the frozen conflict line and prevent localized incidents from triggering broader escalation.
With sanctions already at 42% and our reserves down to 14 weeks, we must urgently secure critical resources to withstand prolonged economic pressure.
Impose tighter controls on dual-use goods and maritime trade to amplify economic strain on Novaris, exploiting their GDP contraction and supply chain fragility.
Launch a domestic war bond campaign to bolster financial resilience, reduce reliance on external aid, and signal long-term economic endurance to both allies and adversaries.
